metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/_cli/help/transition.txt` to the explicit deletion/edit scope in ## What (update it to remove the `require_signature_for_close` line, then regenerate the golden `.out` files).
findings.finding: The plan says 'regenerate golden CLI help (transition__*help.out)' but the actual upstream source `src/rebar/_cli/help/transition.txt` (line 7) also contains `require_signature_for_close` and must itself be edited first — regenerating the golden files without updating the source `.txt` would just re-bake the old text.
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section, change 'regenerate golden CLI help (transition__*help.out)' to 'edit src/rebar/_cli/help/transition.txt to remove the signature-gate line; regenerate golden CLI help (transition__*help.out)'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/_cli/help/transition.txt` to the edit set: remove the `require_signature_for_close` reference on line 7, then regenerate the golden files from the updated source.
findings.finding: `src/rebar/_cli/help/transition.txt` (the SOURCE text that generates the golden help files) contains `require_signature_for_close` on line 7 but is not named as an edit target — the plan mentions regenerating golden CLI help files but not updating the source help text that produces them.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace line 7 of `transition.txt` (the `story/epic close requires a certified signature…` sentence) with a description of the completion-verifier close gate that is actually in use, then re-run the golden capture.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `test_close_gate_story_epic.py:124-131` (`test_gate_on_via_env_var`) to the list of true-path tests to remove/rewrite, as it exercises `REBAR_VERIFY_REQUIRE_SIGNATURE_FOR_CLOSE` directly.
findings.finding: The plan lists deletion targets in `test_close_gate_story_epic.py` at lines 38 and 116 but omits line 126, which sets `REBAR_VERIFY_REQUIRE_SIGNATURE_FOR_CLOSE` via env var — a test that would be left orphaned/broken after the gate machinery is removed.
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'Delete' list under ## What, extend the test_close_gate_story_epic.py entry to include line 126 / the test_gate_on_via_env_var test.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add removal of `'resolve_signature_gate'` from `gates.py`'s `__all__` list (line 25) to the deletion scope in ## What.
findings.finding: The plan names `gates.py:58-80` as the range to delete for `resolve_signature_gate`, but the `__all__` export list at `gates.py:25` explicitly includes `'resolve_signature_gate'` — omitting this from the deletion plan leaves a dangling export that will cause an `AttributeError` at import time or a misleading public API.
findings.suggested_fix: In the deletion bullet for gates.py, append: '+ remove from `__all__` at line 25'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `gates.py:25` (`__all__`) to the edit set: remove `"resolve_signature_gate"` from the list when deleting the function.
findings.finding: `gates.py:__all__` (line 25) exports `resolve_signature_gate` and is not named as an edit target; deleting the function without removing it from `__all__` leaves a stale export that will break `from rebar._commands.gates import *` and potentially type-checkers.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify the disposition of the entire `test_close_gate_story_epic.py` file: either delete it wholesale (preferred, since it is entirely gate-specific) or enumerate every test function that must be removed, including `test_gate_on_via_env_var` (line 124), `test_gate_fails_closed_on_unreadable_config` (line 134), `test_gate_fails_closed_on_malformed_pyproject` (line 146), and `test_gate_force_close_bypasses_with_warning_and_audit_comment` (line 159).
findings.finding: `test_close_gate_story_epic.py` is entirely a true-path test file for the signature gate (all ~10 tests). The plan names only lines 38 and 116 as targets but does not specify whether the whole file is deleted or surgically trimmed. The file has additional unreferenced gate tests: `test_gate_on_via_env_var` (line 124, uses `REBAR_VERIFY_REQUIRE_SIGNATURE_FOR_CLOSE`), `test_gate_fails_closed_on_unreadable_config` (line 134), `test_gate_fails_closed_on_malformed_pyproject` (line 146), `test_gate_force_close_bypasses_with_warning_and_audit_comment` (line 159) — all of which test `require_signature_for_close` behavior and will break or become vacuous after removal.
findings.suggested_fix: Delete `test_close_gate_story_epic.py` entirely, since every test in it exercises the signature gate that is being removed.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Expand the `test_idea_close_bypass.py` rewrite scope to cover lines 26-87 (not just line 27): explicitly specify that `test_idea_story_epic_closes_without_signature` and `test_non_idea_story_still_gated_by_signature` must be rewritten to use `_enable_completion_gate` (or equivalent) as the 'gate ON' discriminator, so the bypass invariant remains proven after the signature gate is removed.
findings.finding: The plan targets only `test_idea_close_bypass.py:27` (the config-key write) for rewrite, but the same file contains two gate-dependent tests at lines 67-87 (`test_idea_story_epic_closes_without_signature`, `test_non_idea_story_still_gated_by_signature`) that call `_enable_signature_gate` (defined at lines 26-27) and rely on the signature gate being ON to form the discriminator that proves the `idea→closed` bypass is real — not just the gate being absent. After deletion, these tests reference a non-existent config key and provide no discriminator unless rewritten against the completion gate or restructured. An implementer following 'rewrite — bypass logic changes' for line 27 only will leave the two dependent tests broken or hollow.
findings.location: What section — `test_idea_close_bypass.py:27` (plan target) vs. actual affected scope lines 26-87
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'What' section, change `test_idea_close_bypass.py:27 (rewrite — bypass logic changes)` to `test_idea_close_bypass.py:26-87 (rewrite: replace _enable_signature_gate + its two call sites with _enable_completion_gate; add a git commit so the completion gate's HEAD binding is satisfied, mirroring the pattern in the completion-gate discriminator tests already in the file)`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `gates.py __all__` to the deletion checklist: remove `"resolve_signature_gate"` from the `__all__` list at `_commands/gates.py:25` when deleting the function.
findings.finding: `gates.py:25` declares `__all__ = ["gate_enabled", "plan_review_precheck", "resolve_signature_gate"]`; the plan deletes `resolve_signature_gate` but does not mention updating `__all__`. This leaves a stale public-API declaration that neither `make lint` nor `make typecheck` will catch (it's a runtime-only list), so it could mislead future readers or external importers.
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'What' section under the `resolve_signature_gate` bullet, append: 'also remove from `__all__` at `gates.py:25`'.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Identify and link the doc-sweep story ticket ID in the plan (or enumerate the specific doc files delegated to it) so the AC1 docs scope exclusion is trackable and its completion verifiable.
findings.finding: The plan's scope exclusion 'Docs handled by the doc-sweep story' delegates cleanup of 6+ doc files to an unnamed/unlinked story. Grepping confirms `require_signature_for_close` appears in docs/manifest-signing.md:60, docs/config.md:96 and :386, docs/migrations.md:142, docs/release-notes.md:46, docs/cli-reference.md:377, docs/llm-framework.md:471 — if the doc-sweep story is absent/cancelled, all these references survive and the AC1 assertion ('removed from schema, parser, and any docs/help') cannot be verified as satisfied.
findings.location: ## Scope — MUST PRESERVE / ## What (docs exclusion) / AC1
findings.suggested_fix: Add a line such as 'Doc files delegated to doc-sweep story <ID>: docs/manifest-signing.md, docs/config.md, docs/migrations.md, docs/release-notes.md, docs/cli-reference.md, docs/llm-framework.md' to the What section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either enumerate the six affected `docs/` files (`manifest-signing.md`, `config.md`, `migrations.md`, `release-notes.md`, `cli-reference.md`, `llm-framework.md`) as explicit edit targets in this ticket, or add a named/linked doc-sweep story ticket ID to the plan so the dependency is trackable.
findings.finding: Six `docs/` files that reference `require_signature_for_close` are not enumerated as edit targets, and the doc-sweep story they are deferred to has no ticket ID or link in the plan.
findings.location: AC line 1: 'removed from schema, parser, and any docs/help (coordinated with the doc-sweep story)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the exact command (e.g. `make regen-golden` or `python -m pytest --snapshot-update`) used to regenerate `transition__*help.out` golden files, so AC4's 'golden help regenerated' claim has a concrete proving step.
findings.finding: The plan directs an executor to 'regenerate golden CLI help (transition__*help.out)' but provides no command or make target for doing so. An executor who doesn't already know the project's golden-regeneration convention would have to stop and investigate.
findings.location: What section (last bullet) / AC4: 'golden help regenerated'
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section or AC4, append: 'Regenerate with `make regen-golden` (or the project-equivalent); confirm `make test` passes afterward.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify what `test_idea_close_bypass.py:27` should assert after the rewrite — e.g. 'the idea close path now bypasses only the completion-verifier gate (not a signature gate), so assert it closes without a signature but still requires completion verification'.
findings.finding: The plan instructs 'rewrite — bypass logic changes' for test_idea_close_bypass.py:27 without specifying what the rewrite should produce. An executor must infer the new expected behavior of the bypass from context, which may differ from the author's intent.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `CLAUDE.md:272` to the edit set: remove or update the `require_signature_for_close` reference when the gate is deleted.
findings.finding: `CLAUDE.md:272` references `require_signature_for_close` as a named config key (visible to developers and AI agents using the repo) and is not in the edit set or the doc-sweep deferral.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the parenthetical `(require_signature_for_close)` reference with a note that the signature gate has been retired, or remove the sentence entirely if the surrounding paragraph still makes sense.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Cite the specific test file(s) or pytest node IDs for the plan-review claim gate and completion-verifier close gate in AC3, so 'existing tests green' is anchored to named tests rather than implied.
findings.finding: AC3's proof claim 'prove with their existing tests green' is plan-text only and doesn't name which test files or test IDs exercise the plan-review claim gate and completion-verifier close gate. If those tests were accidentally deleted or broken during the sweep, `make test` would still report failure, but the AC gives no anchor for a reviewer to confirm the *right* tests are still present and exercising the right paths.
findings.location: AC3: 'prove with their existing tests green'
findings.suggested_fix: Amend AC3 to read: 'prove with e.g. `pytest tests/test_completion_verifier.py tests/test_plan_review_gate.py` (or the actual paths) green.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out plan (or expand-contract note) for the `require_signature_for_close` config-schema removal, even if only: 'field was default-false and project-local; no migration needed; back-out = revert this commit'.
findings.finding: The config schema removal (`require_signature_for_close` field) is a destructive/breaking change to the config contract — any repo that has this key set will get an unknown-field error — but the plan specifies no rollback/back-out path or expand-contract sequence; it only acknowledges the field was never used *in this project*.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-line rollback note under the 'What' or a new 'Rollback' section: 'Back-out: revert this commit; no data migration required because the field defaulted to False and was never persisted in project config. External consumers: unknown field will be silently ignored by the old parser, so the removal is one-directional safe.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Expand AC4 (or add an AC) to explicitly cover trimming flag-specific assertions in `test_config_typed.py`, `test_config_perf.py`, and `test_config_backcompat.py`, distinct from 'true-path tests'.
findings.finding: AC4 uses the term 'true-path tests removed/rewritten' but the 'What' section also describes trimming 'flag-specific assertions in `test_config_typed.py`, `test_config_perf.py`, `test_config_backcompat.py`' — these are config/schema tests, not 'true-path' (happy-path gate-execution) tests. The criterion's verify step does not clearly cover this category of test changes.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria AC4 vs. What section (test_config_typed.py, test_config_perf.py, test_config_backcompat.py)
findings.suggested_fix: Amend AC4 to: '--force-close / --verdict-hash unaffected; signature-gate true-path tests removed/rewritten; flag-specific assertions in config tests (`test_config_typed.py`, `test_config_perf.py`, `test_config_backcompat.py`) trimmed; golden help regenerated.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a story ID / link for the doc-sweep story in this plan, or enumerate the specific doc files that reference `require_signature_for_close` so the handoff is verifiable and complete.
findings.finding: The plan defers all documentation cleanup to a 'doc-sweep story' but provides no story ID, link, or enumeration of which doc files reference `require_signature_for_close`. If the doc-sweep story is not explicitly linked, stale references in user-facing or internal docs could survive this change undetected.
findings.location: ## What / ## Scope — MUST PRESERVE / AC bullet 1 ('coordinated with the doc-sweep story')
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'What' section, add a line such as: 'Doc references to sweep (tracked in story #NNN): list files here (e.g., docs/configuration.md, CLAUDE.md, README).' Alternatively, inline the doc-file list directly in this plan's What section so the sweep is self-contained.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how the config parser/validator handles `require_signature_for_close` appearing in existing config files after removal — either add a graceful unknown-field ignore, emit a deprecation/warning, or document it explicitly as a breaking change in the AC.
findings.finding: The plan removes `require_signature_for_close` from the schema and parser but does not specify how existing configs that contain this field will be treated after the upgrade (strict schema error vs. silent ignore vs. deprecation warning). If the schema validator rejects unknown fields, any user with this key set will get a hard failure after upgrading.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC bullet: 'If the config schema validator rejects unknown fields, add a one-release deprecation shim that emits a warning and ignores `require_signature_for_close` rather than erroring; or confirm the validator already silently ignores unknown fields and add a test in `test_config_backcompat.py` proving graceful handling of a config containing this field.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate `README.md:549` explicitly as an edit target (remove or replace the `verify.require_signature_for_close = true` line in the config example), or explicitly call out that it is covered by the doc-sweep story.
findings.finding: `README.md:549` references `verify.require_signature_for_close = true` in a user-facing TOML config example and is not enumerated as an edit target. README.md lives outside `docs/` so the doc-sweep deferral may not cover it.
findings.location: AC line 1: 'removed from schema, parser, and any docs/help (coordinated with the doc-sweep story)' / `README.md:549`

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
